yield_curve.label: Yield curve spread: 10-year GoC − 3-month T-bill
yield_curve.source: Statistics Canada (Table 10-10-0122), sourced from Bank of Canada
yield_curve.recession_signal: Inversion (spread < 0) historically precedes recession by 6–18 months.

unemployment.label: Unemployment rate, 15+, seasonally adjusted
unemployment.recession_signal: Rising unemployment is the classic coincident-to-lagging recession indicator. The Sahm rule (3-mo avg UR rising ≥0.5ppt above its trailing 12-mo low) has historically triggered early in recessions.

monthly_gdp.label: Monthly real GDP, all industries — YoY % change
monthly_gdp.recession_signal: The coincident indicator par excellence. Two consecutive negative monthly GDP prints is often the first real-time alarm for a recession call.

housing_starts.label: Housing starts, Canada, seasonally adjusted at annual rates
housing_starts.recession_signal: Residential investment is the most cyclically volatile GDP component. Sharp drops in starts typically precede or coincide with broader weakness.

building_permits.label: Building permits, total value, Canada, SA
building_permits.recession_signal: Leading indicator — building permits precede actual housing starts by ~1–2 months, leading broader residential investment.
building_permits.caveat: Table 34-10-0292 only starts 2018-01. For pre-2018 history, splicing with legacy table 34-10-0008 would be required.

vehicle_sales.label: New motor vehicle sales (units) — YoY % change
vehicle_sales.recession_signal: Big-ticket durables — highly sensitive to credit conditions and consumer confidence. Tends to turn slightly ahead of the broader consumer cycle.
vehicle_sales.caveat: Derived from unadjusted levels; YoY strips annual seasonality. Post-2021 data distorted by chip shortages, inventory rebuilds.

retail_sales.label: Retail trade sales, total, SA — YoY % change (nominal)
retail_sales.recession_signal: Nominal retail sales YoY collapsing toward or below zero is a coincident signal of consumer demand weakness.
retail_sales.caveat: Nominal, not deflated. Includes autos and gasoline. Spliced across a 2018-12 table transition; overlap disagreement is <1pp on YoY.

bcpi.label: BoC Commodity Price Index — YoY % change
bcpi.recession_signal: Sharp declines in commodity prices pressure resource-sector employment and investment; historically correlated with Canadian slowdowns.

cpi_headline.label: CPI, all-items, Canada — YoY % change
cpi_headline.recession_signal: Not a recession indicator per se, but essential context: defines the BoC's 1–3% target band and interacts with policy rate decisions.

us_cli.label: OECD Composite Leading Indicator — United States
us_cli.recession_signal: Since ~75% of Canadian exports go to the US, the US CLI is a powerful leading indicator for Canada. Values below 100 and falling historically precede Canadian slowdowns.
us_cli.caveat: Conference Board LEI (first choice) is not freely available on FRED. OECD CLI is the standard free alternative and performs similarly.
